name: Cephaloridine
description: Cephaloridine (or cefaloridine) is a first generation semisynthetic derivative of cephalosporin C. It is unique among cephalosporins in that it exists as a zwitterion.

interactions.name: BCG vaccine
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of BCG vaccine can be decreased when used in combination with Cephaloridine.

interactions.name: Picosulfuric acid
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Picosulfuric acid can be decreased when used in combination with Cephaloridine.

interactions.name: Warfarin
interactions.description: Cephaloridine may increase the anticoagulant activities of Warfarin.

interactions.name: Dicoumarol
interactions.description: Cephaloridine may increase the anticoagulant activities of Dicoumarol.

interactions.name: Phenindione
interactions.description: Cephaloridine may increase the anticoagulant activities of Phenindione.

interactions.name: Phenprocoumon
interactions.description: Cephaloridine may increase the anticoagulant activities of Phenprocoumon.

interactions.name: Acenocoumarol
interactions.description: Cephaloridine may increase the anticoagulant activities of Acenocoumarol.

interactions.name: Ethyl biscoumacetate
interactions.description: Cephaloridine may increase the anticoagulant activities of Ethyl biscoumacetate.

interactions.name: Fluindione
interactions.description: Cephaloridine may increase the anticoagulant activities of Fluindione.

interactions.name: Clorindione
interactions.description: Cephaloridine may increase the anticoagulant activities of Clorindione.

interactions.name: Diphenadione
interactions.description: Cephaloridine may increase the anticoagulant activities of Diphenadione.

interactions.name: Tioclomarol
interactions.description: Cephaloridine may increase the anticoagulant activities of Tioclomarol.

interactions.name: Probenecid
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Cephaloridine can be increased when it is combined with Probenecid.
